title: Error Taxonomy, Wrapping, Retry Decisions, User Recovery, and Exit · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds causal recoverable Pokedex error contract from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of causal recoverable Pokedex error contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Classify input, not found, protocol, timeout, cancellation, transport, decode, invariant, persistence, and internal errors by caller decision.
Wrap context with percent-w while preserving sentinel or typed matching and avoid exposing unstable internal prose as API.
Retry only explicitly transient idempotent work under attempt, time, spacing, cancellation, and fair-use budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6737 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify causal failures, preserve matching, bound retry, and isolate session recovery; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify input, not found, protocol, timeout, cancellation, transport, decode, invariant, persistence, and internal errors by caller decision.
